# Behavior Best Practices — Claude Sessions
Rules of conduct to adopt during sessions. Distinct from `context-file-best-practices.md` which governs the content of CLAUDE.md files.

---

## BB1 — Every Rule Must Come from Real Pain

**Principle:** Files written by humans improve task success by +4%, files generated by LLMs reduce it by 2% (Gloaguen et al., 2026). The difference comes from the origin of each rule — lived experience vs theoretical precaution.

**Test to apply for each line in CLAUDE.md:**

| Question | Answer | Action |
|----------|--------|--------|
| Did I experience the problem this solves? | Yes | Keep |
| Is this a theoretical precaution? | Yes | Remove |
| I don't remember why this is here? | — | Remove |

**Contribution rule:**
- Claude proposes a formulation in the chat
- Roberto validates and writes it into the file
- Claude never modifies CLAUDE.md autonomously

---

## BB2 — Audit Files Are Not Context Files

`context-file-best-practices.md` and `behavior-best-practices.md` are audit reference documents — not context files to load at session start.

Do not reference them in CLAUDE.md. Do not read them at the start of a session. Consult them only during a dedicated CLAUDE.md audit session.

---

## BB3 — When in Doubt, the Code is Always Right

When there is a tension between what CLAUDE.md says and what the code shows, read the source file first. CLAUDE.md may be outdated — the code reflects the current reality.

```
CLAUDE.md says X  ←→  code says Y
                            ↓
                  read the source file
                            ↓
                  trust the code, flag the tension to Roberto
```
